name: accordion
title: Accordion
description: Vertically stacked disclosure sections built on Base UI. Each header is a heading wrapping a native button; multiple panels may be open at once by default (USWDS convention), with an optional single-open mode.
useWhen: FAQ pages where residents scan questions and expand only the answers they need
Progressive disclosure of long supporting content (eligibility details, fee schedules, step-by-step instructions)
Long pages that would otherwise force excessive scrolling past content most users skip
avoidWhen: Content everyone must read (legal notices, deadlines, safety instructions) — hidden content gets missed
Tiny amounts of content that do not need hiding — show the text directly
Switching between peer views of the same content (use tabs)
A single collapsible section inside body copy (use a simple disclosure)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to each trigger in turn — every trigger is a regular tab stop
Enter or Space toggles the focused panel
Arrow keys do not move focus between triggers (Base UI v1.6 follows the updated APG accordion pattern, which removed arrow-key roving focus); this is verified by a test

aria-expanded on each trigger reflects its panel state; aria-controls is present while the panel is open (Base UI unmounts closed panels from the DOM by default, so there is no id to reference while closed — pass keepMounted or hiddenUntilFound on the panel to keep it rendered)
Each open panel is a region labelled by its trigger (role=region + aria-labelledby)
Each trigger is wrapped in a heading element (headingLevel prop, default h3) per the APG accordion pattern
Open state is not conveyed by color alone: the chevron direction flips and aria-expanded updates; the height animation is motion-safe only
